title: Synaxis

infobox.value: ([Greek](/wiki/Greek_language "Greek language"): σύναξις "gathering"; [Slavonic](/wiki/Church_Slavonic "Church Slavonic"): собор, *sobor*) is a liturgical assembly in [Eastern Christianity](/wiki/Eastern_Christianity "Eastern Christianity") (the [Eastern Orthodox Church](/wiki/Eastern_Orthodox_Church "Eastern Orthodox Church") and those [Eastern Catholic Churches](/wiki/Eastern_Catholic_Churches "Eastern Catholic Churches") which follow the [Byzantine Rite](/wiki/Byzantine_Rite "Byzantine Rite")).

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Liturgical assembly in Eastern Christianity

For the genus of moths, see [Synaxis (moth)](/wiki/Synaxis_(moth) "Synaxis (moth)").

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/f/f5/Synaxis_of_the_Theotokos_%2815th_c.%2C_P.Korin%27s_house-museum%29.jpg/250px-Synaxis_of_the_Theotokos_%2815th_c.%2C_P.Korin%27s_house-museum%29.jpg)](/wiki/File:Synaxis_of_the_Theotokos_(15th_c.,_P.Korin%27s_house-museum).jpg)

[Icon](/wiki/Icon "Icon") of the Synaxis of the [Theotokos](/wiki/Theotokos "Theotokos") ([Pskov](/wiki/Pskov "Pskov"), 17th century)

A **synaxis** ([Greek](/wiki/Greek_language "Greek language"): σύναξις "gathering"; [Slavonic](/wiki/Church_Slavonic "Church Slavonic"): собор, *sobor*) is a liturgical assembly in [Eastern Christianity](/wiki/Eastern_Christianity "Eastern Christianity") (the [Eastern Orthodox Church](/wiki/Eastern_Orthodox_Church "Eastern Orthodox Church") and those [Eastern Catholic Churches](/wiki/Eastern_Catholic_Churches "Eastern Catholic Churches") which follow the [Byzantine Rite](/wiki/Byzantine_Rite "Byzantine Rite")).

## Synaxes of feast days

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Synaxis&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Synaxes of feast days")]

In [Constantinople](/wiki/Constantinople "Constantinople"), the clergy and faithful would often gather together on specific [feast days](/wiki/Feast_day "Feast day") at a church dedicated to the saint of that day for liturgical celebrations. These gatherings were referred to as *synaxes*. These synaxes came to have services written specifically for them. A Synaxis often occurs on the day following a [Major Feast Day](/wiki/Great_Feast "Great Feast") and is in honor of saints who participated in the event. For example, services on the Feast of [Theophany](/wiki/Epiphany_(holiday) "Epiphany (holiday)") (the revelation of the [Trinity](/wiki/Trinity "Trinity") at the [Baptism of Jesus](/wiki/Baptism_of_Jesus "Baptism of Jesus") in the [Jordan](/wiki/River_Jordan "River Jordan")) would be held at [Hagia Sophia](/wiki/Hagia_Sophia "Hagia Sophia"); then, the next day, a Synaxis was observed in honor of [St. John the Forerunner](/wiki/John_the_Baptist "John the Baptist") at the church dedicated to him. Over time, the synaxes came into general use and are now celebrated in every church.[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*]

Synaxis can also refer to a common commemoration of a number of saints in a single service, such as the Synaxis of the [Seventy Apostles](/wiki/Seventy_Apostles "Seventy Apostles"). Each individual saint may have his or her own separate feast day, but they are all commemorated together on their synaxis.[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*]

Most synaxes are observed as [fixed feasts](/wiki/Eastern_Orthodox_liturgical_calendar "Eastern Orthodox liturgical calendar"), being celebrated on the same calendar date year after year, though some occur on the nearest Sunday to a particular date. Other synaxes are celebrated on the [Paschal cycle](/wiki/Paschal_cycle "Paschal cycle"), moving backward or forward in the calendar according to the date of [Pascha](/wiki/Easter "Easter") (Easter) that year.[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*]

### Rite of Constantinople

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Synaxis&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Rite of Constantinople")]

The following are Synaxes which are universally observed in the [Rite of Constantinople](/wiki/Rite_of_Constantinople "Rite of Constantinople"):

* Synaxis of the Theotokos (December 26)[[1]](#cite_note-1)
* [Synaxis of the Seventy Apostles](http://ocafs.oca.org/FeastSaintsViewer.asp?SID=4&ID=1&FSID=100017) (January 4)
* [Synaxis of the Forerunner](http://ocafs.oca.org/FeastSaintsViewer.asp?SID=4&ID=1&FSID=100109) (January 7)
* [Synaxis of the Holy Fathers of the Sixth Ecumenical Council](http://ocafs.oca.org/FeastSaintsViewer.asp?SID=4&ID=1&FSID=100284) (January 23)
* [Synaxis of the Three Great Hierarchs](http://ocafs.oca.org/FeastSaintsViewer.asp?SID=4&ID=1&FSID=100350) (January 30)
* [Synaxis of the Archangel Gabriel](http://ocafs.oca.org/FeastSaintsViewer.asp?SID=4&ID=1&FSID=100886) (March 26 and [July 13](http://ocafs.oca.org/FeastSaintsViewer.asp?SID=4&ID=1&FSID=102017))
* [Synaxis of the Holy Fathers of the Second Ecumenical Council](http://ocafs.oca.org/FeastSaintsViewer.asp?SID=4&ID=1&FSID=101462) (May 22)
* [Synaxis of the Holy Fathers of the First Ecumenical Council](http://ocafs.oca.org/FeastSaintsViewer.asp?SID=4&ID=1&FSID=43) (Sunday before [Pentecost](/wiki/Pentecost "Pentecost"))
* [Synaxis of All Saints](http://ocafs.oca.org/FeastSaintsViewer.asp?SID=4&ID=1&FSID=48) (first Sunday after Pentecost)
* [Synaxis of the Twelve Apostles](http://ocafs.oca.org/FeastSaintsViewer.asp?SID=4&ID=1&FSID=101711) (June 30)
* [Synaxis of the Holy Fathers of the Fifth Ecumenical Council](http://ocafs.oca.org/FeastSaintsViewer.asp?SID=4&ID=1&FSID=102090) (July 25)
* [Synaxis of the Holy Fathers of the Third Ecumenical Council](http://ocafs.oca.org/FeastSaintsViewer.asp?SID=4&ID=1&FSID=102555) (September 9)
* [Synaxis of the Holy Fathers of the Seventh Ecumenical Council](http://ocafs.oca.org/FeastSaintsViewer.asp?SID=4&ID=1&FSID=70) (Sunday on or after October 8)
* [Synaxis of the Holy Unmercenaries](http://ocafs.oca.org/FeastSaintsViewer.asp?SID=4&ID=1&FSID=103133) (November 1)
* [Synaxis of the Archangel Michael and the Other Bodiless Powers](http://ocafs.oca.org/FeastSaintsViewer.asp?SID=4&ID=1&FSID=103244) (November 8)

### Local observance

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Synaxis&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Local observance")]

There are also synaxes which have been composed for local observance:

* [Synaxis of the Saints of Kostroma](http://ocafs.oca.org/FeastSaintsViewer.asp?SID=4&ID=1&FSID=100285) (January 23)
* [Synaxis of the Hierarchs of Novgorod](http://ocafs.oca.org/FeastSaintsViewer.asp?SID=4&ID=1&FSID=100479) (February 10, October 4, and the third Sunday after Pentecost)
* [Synaxis of the Venerable Fathers of the Kiev Caves Lavra](http://ocafs.oca.org/FeastSaintsViewer.asp?SID=4&ID=1&FSID=100521) (second Sunday of [Great Lent](/wiki/Great_Lent "Great Lent"))
* [Synaxis of the Rostov and Yaroslavl](http://ocafs.oca.org/FeastSaintsViewer.asp?SID=4&ID=1&FSID=101468) (May 23)
* [Synaxis of the Saints of Ryzan](http://ocafs.oca.org/FeastSaintsViewer.asp?SID=4&ID=1&FSID=101688) (June 10)
* [Synaxis of the Saints of Siberia](http://ocafs.oca.org/FeastSaintsViewer.asp?SID=4&ID=1&FSID=101689) (June 10)
* [Synaxis of the Saints of Belarus](http://ocafs.oca.org/FeastSaintsViewer.asp?FSID=103750) (June 14)
* [Synaxis of the Saints of North America](http://ocafs.oca.org/FeastSaintsViewer.asp?SID=4&ID=1&FSID=49) (second Sunday after Pentecost)
* [Synaxis of All Saints of Pskov](http://ocafs.oca.org/FeastSaintsViewer.asp?SID=4&ID=1&FSID=51) (third Sunday after Pentecost)
* [Synaxis of the Saints of Vladimir](http://ocafs.oca.org/FeastSaintsViewer.asp?SID=4&ID=1&FSID=101796) (June 23)
* Synaxis of the Saints of [Tver](/wiki/Tver "Tver") (first Sunday after June 29th))
* [Synaxis of the Saints of Radonezh](http://ocafs.oca.org/FeastSaintsViewer.asp?SID=4&ID=1&FSID=101949) (July 6)
* [Synaxis of the Saints of Smolensk](http://ocafs.oca.org/FeastSaintsViewer.asp?SID=4&ID=1&FSID=206391) (Sunday closest to July 23)
* [Synaxis of the Saints of Solovki Monastery](http://ocafs.oca.org/FeastSaintsViewer.asp?SID=4&ID=1&FSID=102256) (August 9)
* Synaxis of All Saints of [Moscow](/wiki/Moscow "Moscow") (Sunday before August 26)
* [Synaxis of the Venerable Fathers of the Far Caves in Kiev](http://ocafs.oca.org/FeastSaintsViewer.asp?SID=4&ID=1&FSID=102418) (August 28)
* Synaxis of All Saints of [Serbia](/wiki/Serbia "Serbia") (August 28)
* [Synaxis of the Serbian Hierarchs](http://ocafs.oca.org/FeastSaintsViewer.asp?SID=4&ID=1&FSID=102428) (August 30)
* [Synaxis of the Saints of Tula](http://ocafs.oca.org/FeastSaintsViewer.asp?SID=4&ID=1&FSID=102702) (September 22)
* [Synaxis of All Saints of Alaska](http://ocafs.oca.org/FeastSaintsViewer.asp?SID=4&ID=1&FSID=102712) (September 24)
* [Synaxis of the Venerable Fathers of the Near Caves in Kiev](http://ocafs.oca.org/FeastSaintsViewer.asp?SID=4&ID=1&FSID=102757) (September 28)
* [Synaxis of the Hierarchs of Kazan](http://ocafs.oca.org/FeastSaintsViewer.asp?SID=4&ID=1&FSID=102852) (October 4)
* [Synaxis of the Hierarchs of Moscow](http://ocafs.oca.org/FeastSaintsViewer.asp?SID=4&ID=1&FSID=102871) (October 5)
* [Synaxis of the Saints of Volhynia](http://ocafs.oca.org/FeastSaintsViewer.asp?SID=4&ID=1&FSID=102923) (October 10)
* [Synaxis of the Venerable Fathers of Optina](http://ocafs.oca.org/FeastSaintsViewer.asp?SID=4&ID=1&FSID=103801) (October 11)
* Synaxis of the Enlighteners of [Karelia](/wiki/Karelia "Karelia") (Saturday between October 31 and November 6)[[2]](#cite_note-2)
* [Synaxis of the Saints of Georgia](http://ocafs.oca.org/FeastSaintsViewer.asp?SID=4&ID=1&FSID=103910) (December 11)
